note: design↔live element correspondence for the Generative Surfaces v2 canvas tab strip. Design side = the vendored `Generative Surfaces v2.dc.html` mock's surface-canvas tab strip (`.sheet-tabs` / `.sheet-tab`, copilot.css) — the named-surfaces-per-run strip B1 introduces. Live side = the REAL chat-surface `TcTabs` the Run cockpit mounts (`ThreadCanvas` → `TcTabs`, rendered with `role=tablist` / `role=tab` and a lime pinned dot). `TcTabs` uses inline styles (no class hooks), so the live anchors target the ARIA roles + testids. The active-tab treatment (design `.sheet-tab[data-on=true]` background/border vs live active border-bottom accent) is a known stylistic divergence flagged expectDivergence — TcTabs is the shipped kit component, not a reimplementation of the mock's pill-tab. VENDOR PROVENANCE: the mock is a design-compiler template (`index.dc.html`, `{{ }}` placeholders + `<sc-if>`); rendering the DESIGN side to comparable HTML needs the design compiler / DesignSync (Claude Design project ceb081f6 per SDR §9), so the 0-HIGH computed-style compare is an INTEGRATION-time step — see out/README.md. This file + the render-live-surfaces-v2 test stage the live side and the mapping so that run is a mechanical follow-up, not a re-derivation.

elements.label: tab.active
elements.expectDivergence: live TcTabs marks the active tab with a bottom accent border (design-system --color-accent), the mock uses a filled pill (panel2 bg + line border) — the shipped kit treatment, intended

elements.label: tab.dot
elements.expectDivergence: the mock's neutral status dot vs TcTabs' lime pinned-dot — both are the small round surface-state indicator, colored per each system's tokens
